name: Uniform Linear Array
description: A uniform linear array consists of identical antenna elements arranged in a straight line with equal spacing and uniform amplitude/phase excitation. This configuration provides directional radiation patterns with controllable beamwidth and sidelobe levels through proper element spacing and excitation.

num_elements.name: Number of Elements
num_elements.description: Total number of identical elements in the linear array

array_length.name: Array Length
array_length.description: Total physical length of the array

progressive_phase.name: Progressive Phase Shift
progressive_phase.description: Phase difference between adjacent elements for beam steering

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting element type, determining spacing for desired radiation characteristics, calculating excitation coefficients, and optimizing for specific performance requirements.

steps.title: Element Selection
steps.description: Choose appropriate radiating element based on frequency and polarization requirements

steps.title: Spacing Determination
steps.description: Set element spacing to avoid grating lobes while achieving desired beamwidth

steps.title: Array Factor Calculation
steps.description: Compute array factor for uniform amplitude and progressive phase

steps.title: Beamwidth Estimation
steps.description: Calculate half-power beamwidth for broadside radiation

steps.title: Directivity Calculation
steps.description: Determine maximum directivity for uniform illumination

steps.title: Impedance Matching
steps.description: Design feed network to match array impedance to transmission line

steps.title: Sidelobe Optimization
steps.description: Apply amplitude tapering if lower sidelobes are required

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use mutual coupling matrix with self and mutual impedances between elements, account for feed network transformation
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Pattern multiplication of element pattern and array factor, include mutual coupling effects on element patterns
keyAssumptions: Progressive phase shift for beam steering

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 6
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 7
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 11
